Description: RQ100	Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reasoning: The rows below are trace links, not assertion proof. There is no explicit evidence in tests or code that requirements are managed in a 3rd-party RM tool; therefore the supplied evidence is insufficient to verify the requirement.

Description: RQ101	Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reasoning: None of the provided candidate tests or supporting code reference management of non-requirement artifacts in 3rd-party tools (Rational Rose or Clear case). Candidate tests focus on trace operations, link state changes, requirement lifecycle events, and subscriber registration behavior. There is no evidence of any test asserting (1) that artifacts are persisted to or synchronized with Rational Rose or Clear case, (2) that the system speaks the required APIs/protocols for those tools, or (3) error/failure behaviors when interacting with such tools.

Description: RQ102	Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reasoning: Direct positive assertion that a registered subscriber can establish a trace, satisfying the allow clause for the exercised scenario.

Description: RQ102	Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reasoning: Direct negative assertion that an unregistered subscriber is prevented from creating a trace, satisfying the prohibition clause for the exercised scenario.

Description: RQ103	A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reasoning: Contains explicit step and postcondition stating the subscriber registers and is registered under the subscriber manager, directly addressing the requirement that a user shall register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103	A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reasoning: Exercises a negative/abort registration path and asserts removal, but does not assert successful registration or the postcondition of being registered under the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ104	On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: The test explicitly performs the registration action and asserts the postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Direct affirmative steps and postcondition assert that a subscriber establishes a trace and that the trace exists, covering the core happy path for trace creation with a UML artifact.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Provides an explicit negative case (non-registered subscriber) demonstrating failure to establish a trace, but does not cover artifact-originated subscription or other required behaviors.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Shows consequences of an existing subscription (notifications) which corroborates subscription semantics but does not demonstrate subscription/trace creation itself.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Directly asserts placement for a subscriber artifact but does not prove the universal requirement 'Each artifact'.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Asserts enforcement when a subscriber fails to register, supporting operational control but not demonstrating placement for all artifact types or other required behaviors.

Description: RQ107	The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reasoning: The test issues the delete action and asserts the subscription no longer exists, directly verifying the requirement.

Reasoning: Directly asserts the subscriber manager performs registration and the postcondition that it is registered.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Contains 'At least one subscriber manager is online at its designated IP address and port.', 'An event is published in real time', and 'An event notification is sent to the relevant subscriber manager.' which directly assert the requirement's condition, action, and timing.

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Shows push send and receipt ('An event notification is sent...' and 'The relevant subscriber manager receives the event notification.') but lacks the explicit online precondition and explicit 'real time' wording.

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Reasoning: Explicitly describes server sending notifications to subscriber managers ('...sends it to each relevant subscriber manager.') demonstrating push behavior, but lacks an explicit online precondition and explicit 'real time' statement.

Description: RQ110	Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reasoning: The test's precondition (manager offline and event server has stored an undelivered notification), step (manager comes online), and postcondition ('has successfully downloaded the relevant event notifications from the event server') directly verify the pull-on-start behavior.

Description: RQ132	For speculative events; the eventserver shall construct a composite event message.
Reasoning: No candidate tests were provided and the available test descriptions (TC159, TC151, TC152, TC150, TC149) do not contain assertions that the eventserver constructs or assembles a composite event message. The traceability analysis explicitly states receipt, parsing, categorization, subscriber processing, and message storage are covered but composition, content/schema, aggregation, error handling, and forwarding of a composite message are unverified.
